sorted(glob.glob('shelf_audit_*.csv'))], ignore_index=True)\nsa = sa.merge(visits[['visit_id','rep_id']], on='visit_id', how='left')\ng = sa.groupby('rep_id')\nrepq = pd.DataFrame({\n g['promo_stand'].mean(),'pos_mat': g['pos_materials'].mean()})\nz = (repq - repq.mean())/repq.std()\nrepq['cz'] = z.mean(axis=1)\n\n# Sales per store over the year\nsales = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f, usecols=['store_id','units_sold','revenue']) for f in sorted(glob.glob('sales_transaction_*.csv'))], ignore_index=True)\nstore_sales = sales.groupby('store_id').agg(units=('units_sold','sum'), revenue=('revenue','sum'))\n\n# Traffic per store\ntraffic = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f, usecols=['store_id','customer_count']) for f in sorted(glob.glob('store_traffic_*.csv'))], ignore_index=True)\nstore_traffic = traffic.groupby('store_id')['customer_count'].sum().rename('traffic')\n\n# Visit-weighted rep quality per store\nv = visits.merge(repq[['cz']], on='rep_id', The answer is written to `/app/answer.json`.\n\n## Summary\n\n**Underperforming reps: `[3, 8, 17, 31, 45, 49, 64, 68, 75, 98]`**\n\n### Approach\n\nThe dataset describes 100 reps (5 per region × 20 regions) servicing 500 stores, with a year of operational and sales data. The key structural facts that shaped the analysis:\n\n1. **Reps only service stores in their own region**, and **each store is shared by ~3.5 reps** — so store sales *cannot* be attributed to a single rep.\n\n2. **Raw sales are a confounded trap.** The prompt warns that sales depend on store size, traffic, location, competition, and promotions. I confirmed this empirically: store-level sales efficiency (units per customer) has **~zero correlation (0.004)** with the work quality of the servicing reps. Judging reps by raw sales would be wrong — external factors dominate, and the per-store rep dilution washes out any rep signal.\n\n3. **Work efficiency must be measured from what the rep actually controls** — the operational outcomes tied directly to each rep via `visit_id`: shelf facings, planogram compliance, displays (endcap/checkout/promo-stand/POS materials), and visit completion. These are exactly the responsibilities listed in the prompt (availability, display, placement, planogram execution).\n\n### Finding\n\nA composite z-score across **7 independent controllable work metrics** produced one tight cluster of 10 reps sitting **1.8–2.5σ below the mean**, then a **1.5σ jump** to everyone else (largest within-cluster gap is only 0.28σ). The same 10 reps appear at the bottom of *every* metric independently — facings, planogram compliance, all four display types, and visit completion rate — while showing **normal visit volume and duration** (they aren't lazy; their execution is poor). Each flagged rep is alone in a distinct region (10 of 20 regions affected), and hire dates are mixed, ruling out tenure as the cause.\n\nThe convergence of all signals on the same 10 reps, separated by a clean statistical gap, makes this a high-confidence identification."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":211,"cache_read_input_tokens":56744,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":211,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"aee5c038-bb25-4e2d-82a2-1c72467d879b","uuid":"34cc20fa-99e4-461f-a80a-ac93f0f8fb79","request_id":"req_011Cbf1BryjxzT37awezvfua"}
